concerns.text: In the Abstract, the phrase 'Yet practical training recipes remain insufficiently explored' is slightly passive. Consider 'Yet practical training recipes remain underexplored' for conciseness. (Line 14)

In Section 1 (Introduction), the sentence 'It remains unclear which types of long-context data to synthesize...' is a fragment following a comma. It should be a new sentence or joined with a semicolon. (Line 42)

In Section 4 (Data Curation), the phrase 'We use long documents as data sources because they provide...' is repetitive with the previous sentence. Consider merging or rephrasing for better flow. (Line 112)

In Section 5 (Data Mixture), the transition 'Having identified long-document VQA as an effective data source, we now study...' is good, but the subsequent list of 'three key design choices' could be more clearly enumerated for readability. (Line 205)

In Section 6 (Final Results), the phrase 'These gains also transfer to broader multimodal long-context tasks...' is slightly vague. Consider specifying 'generalize' instead of 'transfer' for precision. (Line 310)
